=== Plan ↔ Backlog Mapping
This table maps our phased refactor/feature plan to the backlog feature entries (FTR-*). It reflects current status and the intended landing phase.
| Feature (FTR) | Brief | Phase | Status
| FTR-TEST-HARNESS | Dual markers + Make targets (IR/legacy) | Phase 1 | Done
| FTR-COLUMN-MAP | Column-aware validations via composer header_map | Phase 2 | Done
| FTR-IR-WRITEPATH | IR write pipeline (compose→passes→render) | Phase 2 | Done (refining)
| FTR-STYLE-THEMES | Style pass v1: header fill, bold, freeze, autofilter | Phase 3 | Next
| FTR-NAMED-RANGES | Named ranges for tables/columns; basis for formulas | Phase 3 | Planned
| FTR-META-PERSISTENCE | Persist meta in hidden _meta sheet | Phase 3 | Planned
| FTR-MULTIHEADER | Multi-row headers (MultiIndex → Excel rows/merges) | Phase 3 | Planned
| FTR-FORMULA-PROVIDERS | Helper columns, FK lookups (XLOOKUP/INDEX-MATCH) | Phase 4 | Planned
| FTR-LEGEND-BLOCKS | Legend/config blocks & multiple tables per sheet | Phase 4 | Planned
| FTR-IR-READPATH | Excel → IR → Frames (+meta) | Phase 5 | Planned
| FTR-COMPACT-TRANSFORMS | XRef ⇄ Cross-Table ⇄ Compact codes (bijective) | Phase 6 | Concept ready
| FTR-VALIDATION-COMPACT | Generate allowed compact combos (cap + policy) | Phase 6 | Concept ready
| FTR-META-SCHEMA-V2 | Consolidated schema (compact, partition, validations) | Phase 6 | Planned
| FTR-PARTITION-BY-DOMAIN | Split/merge by component attribute to reduce merge conflicts | Phase 7 | Concept ready
| FTR-JSON-BUNDLER | Nested JSON via synthetic tables & bundling | Phase 7 | Planned
| FTR-PERF-LIMITS | Safety caps for combinatorial lists/validations | Cross-cutting | Planned
| FTR-ODS-RENDERER | ODS adapter using the same IR (graceful degrade) | Later | Planned
==== Notes
* **Phase 1–2** are complete (core IR backbone, column-aware validations, test harness).
* **Phase 3** focuses on parity-visible improvements (styling, named ranges, meta sheet, multi-headers).
* **Phase 4** brings helper columns (multi-column enrichment) and simple formulas using named ranges.
* **Phase 5** adds the IR read path for full roundtrip parity.
* **Phase 6+** delivers higher-level domain features (compact transforms) and schema consolidation.
* **Cross-cutting** items (perf limits) will be applied where relevant during implementation.
